business.headline: Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

India's best defence is to electrify everything, says Sagar AdaniSagar Adani said India's long-term constraints, water security, food systems, and digital growth, all run into the same bottleneck: reliable, affordable energy.

No petrol or diesel shortage, govt assures day after PM's 'save fuel' appealDuring an inter-ministerial briefing on the situation in West Asia, government officials said India has enough fuel stocks and has taken steps to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict in the region.

IT employees' union urges govt to back work-from-home following PM's appealIn a letter dated May 11, 2026, NITES urged the Ministry of Labour and Employment to direct IT and digitally deliverable service sectors to implement work-from-home arrangements for a suitable period in the national interest.

Sensex ends 1,313 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Titan down 7%The BSE Sensex plunged 1,312.91 points, or 1.70%, to close at 76,015.28, while the Nifty 50 fell 360.30 points, or 1.49%, to settle at 23,815.85, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.

Government has no plans to raise gold, silver import duties: ReportThe clarification comes a day after PM Modi urged citizens to reduce fuel consumption, avoid unnecessary foreign travel and postpone gold purchases as India prepares for the economic impact of rising oil prices linked to the Iran conflict and fears surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.

May 2026 tax deadlines: These key TDS, TCS dates taxpayers should not missIf you have tax-related work pending, May 2026 is a month you may not want to ignore. Several key TDS and TCS deadlines are approaching, and missing them could lead to late fees, penalties or unnecessary stress.

8th Pay Commission: Why govt employees want family unit revised from 3 to 5As part of its continuing exclusive and in-depth coverage of the 8th Pay Commission, IndiaToday.in has learnt that employee bodies are now pushing for the current '3-family-unit' formula to be revised to '5 family units'.

What happens if Indians stop buying gold for a year like PM Modi asked?Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to postpone non-essential gold purchases amid rising oil prices and West Asia tensions. Economists say any sustained fall in demand could ease pressure on the current account deficit, rupee and forex reserves.

PM Modi's call hits gold prices: Should investors worry?For years, gold has been the go-to safe investment for Indian families during uncertain times. But after PM Modi's call to cut back on gold buying, prices took a hit. As global worries rise and the yellow metal weakens, should investors start worrying?

The math behind PM Modi's gold, petrol and foreign travel advisoryPM Modi's latest advisory is not just about cutting fuel use or avoiding gold purchases. It is really about India's current account deficit, rising crude oil prices and protecting the economy from external shocks. Here is why your fuel bill, gold jewellery and foreign trip suddenly matter more than you think.

Titan shares fall over 6%: Why jewellery stocks are under pressure todayTitan and other jewellery stocks fell sharply after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The remark added to concerns over gold demand, imports and pressure on the rupee amid elevated crude prices.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.
Sensex plunges 800 points at open as crude oil jumps on US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 847.62 points to 76,480.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 249.65 points to 23,926.50 as of 9:27 am.

Will stock market crash today after PM Modi's warning on fuel and gold buying?Markets are expected to react to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's remarks asking citizens to conserve fuel and avoid buying gold for weddings for one year amid pressure from rising global energy prices.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayOnce upon a time, Rs 1 crore felt like enough to secure life after retirement. Today, many Indians believe even Rs 10–15 crore may not be enough. I spoke to families to find out what retirement means to them today, and why expectations are rising.

US proposes H-1B minimum wage hike, likely to make hiring foreign workers costlierThe US Department of Labour has proposed sharply higher prevailing wages for H-1B and related visa programmes. The draft rule has drawn both support and opposition ahead of the May 26 comment deadline.

Can motor insurers reject claims for reporting accidents late?Handcrafted ByOur Editors In the fifth story in our series Motor Mess, we spoke to experts to understand why delayed accident reporting often leads to insurance claim rejections, the mistakes policyholders should avoid and what options remain if such claims are rejected.

Two inspections, one pattern: Labour off the booksIn the sixth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, multiple factory inspections conducted in 1988 expose recurring patterns of undocumented labour, hidden wage records and statutory evasion.

entertainment.headline: Miami cops sue Ben Affleck and Matt Damon over alleged reputation damage in The Rip Miami-Dade officers Jason Smith and Jonathan Santana have sued Ben Affleck and Matt Damon's firm over The Rip. They say the film's true-events framing, and fictional conduct damaged their reputations.

Mohanlal on Vijay's superstardom: There is an unknowable magic During the promotions of Drishyam 3, Mohanlal spoke about Vijay's charisma and the magic that helped him connect with audiences. His remarks came as Vijay assumed office as the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu following Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam's sweeping 2026 poll win.

Arjun Kapoor celebrates 14 years of Ishaqzaade: Parma changed everything for me Arjun Kapoor marked 14 years in Bollywood by revisiting Ishaqzaade on Instagram. His post, featuring sketches from the film, renewed attention on the role that launched his career.

Tovino Thomas, Nazriya reveal new film title amid Barcelona vs Real Madrid clash Malayalam actors Tovino Thomas and Nazriya Nazim unveiled Gracias El Clasico in Barcelona during El Clasico. The launch, which took place during the Barcelona vs Real Madrid football match, marks their first film together and promises a blend of love, family dynamics and football.

Anupam Kher revisits iconic DDLJ shooting spot, makes special appeal to government Anupam Kher returned to Apta Railway Station and revisited the iconic Dilwale Dhulaniya Le Jayengey shooting spot after 32 years. He said the visit felt frozen in time and urged its preservation as cinematic heritage.

Who is Siddharth Gupta, actor playing Lord Krishna in Krishnavataram Part 1? Siddharth Gupta has grabbed the spotlight with his portrayal of Lord Krishna in Krishnavataram Part 1: The Heart. Here's all you need to know about the actor.

Director Mudassar Aziz defends Pati Patni Aur Woh Do amid infidelity criticism Director Mudassar Aziz has rejected criticism that Pati Patni Aur Woh Do trivialises extramarital affairs and promotes infidelity. He said the sequel centres on marriage, misunderstandings and a husband without a roving eye.

Remarkably Bright Creatures author on Netflix adaptation, Sally Field, Lewis Pullman Author Shelby Van Pelt said seeing her novel, Remarkably Bright Creatures, become a Netflix film feels surreal. She said the screen version works because it keeps the novel's emotional core intact.

Manjummel Boys fame Chidambaram locks June release for Balan: The Boy Chidambaram's Balan: The Boy is set for a June 2026 theatrical release, in five languages. The announcement comes just after its Cannes market screening was confirmed, adding momentum to the emotional drama.

Vijay and Udhayanidhi's old pic goes viral as they get set for Assembly face-off Vijay and Udhayanidhi Stalin have been film industry colleagues for years. They will now face off in the Assembly, with TVK's Vijay as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and Udhaynidhi as Leader of Opposition. An old photograph of the two actors-turned-politicians has now gone viral.

Abhishek Bachchan reacts to reports about actor fees for Raja Shivaji Abhishek Bachchan said reports about him charging for Raja Shivaji were false and stressed that his appearance was rooted in belief. He said the film mattered because of his bond with Riteish and Genelia Deshmukh and their approach beyond box office figures.

Vijay did true justice to Jana Nayagan title by becoming chief minister: Producer In an exclusive interview with India Today, producer Venkat K Narayana spoke about Vijay's swearing-in and Jana Nayagan's delay. He said public backing, discipline and pending censor clearance helped Vijay's move into government.

Vijay Deverakonda shows off stallion riding in new Ranbaali making video The makers of Ranbaali unveiled a birthday poster and a glimpse showcasing Vijay Deverakonda's warrior transformation. The clip features Vijay undergoing intense horse-riding training with massive stallions.

Palak Tiwari says Salman Khan's Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan failure didn't impact her Palak Tiwari opened up about the failure of Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan, saying she never saw it as her personal setback because the film belonged entirely to Salman Khan in the public imagination. The actor also spoke about breaking stereotypes with Lukkhe and earning opportunities through auditions.

Ananya Panday-Lakshya's Chand Mera Dil trailer hints at a modern, toxic love story The trailer of Chand Mera Dil introduces Ananya Panday's Chandni and Lakshya's Aarav in a relationship marked by love, conflict and repeated apologies. The glimpses from the film hint at a modern, toxic romance.

Desi Bling trailer: Tejasswi-Karan caught in Dubai's high-society drama The trailer of Desi Bling promises glamour, luxury, and plenty of drama within Dubai's elite Indian social circle. Actors Karan Kundrra and Tejasswi Prakash's relationship takes centre stage in the show, which is set to premiere on Netflix this month.

Mohanlal reacts to Patriot criticism: I liked the movie despite mixed reactions Mohanlal said he chose Patriot because its story felt like a different experience when he first heard it. With the film receiving mixed reactions, he added that success cannot be predicted and that sincerity matters most.

Kunal Kemmu fought the thief, I cried in corner: Soha recalls burglary incident Soha Ali Khan recalled a burglary attempt at her home and said Kunal Kemmu fought the intruder. Her account reminded of how Saif Ali Khan was attacked during a robbery attempt at his home earlier this year.

Felt targeted, cornered: Sunil Pal on Samay-Ranveer episode of Kapil Sharma's show Sunil Pal claimed the audience was told not to laugh at his jokes on The Great Indian Kapil Show featuring Samay Raina and Ranveer Allahbadia. The comedian alleged his stand-up segment was dropped and accused Navjot Singh Sidhu of favouring Samay.

general news.headline: TMC claims SIR deletions impacted Bengal poll results, Supreme Court respondsThe Supreme Court said Mamata Banerjee and others could file fresh applications over electoral roll deletions in West Bengal. The hearing centred on whether Special Intensive Revision disputes may have affected election results.

Why TVK's White Paper could be a red flag for CM VijayAs C Joseph Vijay took oath as the chief minister of Tamil Nadu, he highlighted the state's debt burden of Rs 10 lakh crore and promised a White Paper. From the very stage, he also signed an executive order on free electricity of up to 200 units. Will the TVK's White Paper act as a guardrail for Vijay's poll promises?

Folded hands, hugs and bouquets: Vijay meets ex-Tamil Nadu CM MK StalinA day after taking oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, TVK chief C Joseph Vijay met former CM MK Stalin and Udhayanidhi in Chennai ahead of the crucial May 13 Assembly floor test.

BSF to get full control of border fencing in 45 days: Bengal CM at 1st Cabinet meetIn his first Cabinet meeting, West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced that the land transfer process to the BSF to complete fencing the Bangladesh border will be done in 45 days. Adhikari also announced the full implementation of central schemes in the state.

Two leaves, two factions: AIADMK battles for survival in Tamil Nadu resetSeparate arrivals by AIADMK MLAs at the new Assembly session underlined the challenge to Edappadi Palaniswami's leadership after another defeat. The unrest has revived talk of a split, with Vijay's TVK poised to gain from any rupture.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

I-PAC sabotaged TMC's poll campaign, can land Mamata in jail, claim party leadersAfter the Trinamool Congress's defeat in the West Bengal Assembly election, several party leaders have attacked consultancy firm I-PAC, alleging it hijacked and sabotaged its electoral campaign. A former TMC spokesperson even claimed that former CM Mamata Banerjee's defence of I-PAC during ED raids, could land her in jail.

135 questions in NEET practice bank match actual exam, Rajasthan cops probeConcerns over a possible NEET 2026 paper leak have grown after investigators found that a handwritten "guess paper" circulated before the exam allegedly matched questions worth nearly 600 marks in the actual NEET paper held on May 3. The NTA, which conducts NEET, said it was aware of the alleged malpractice and awaiting the probe outcome.

Is WFH only a matter of time? PM's appeal underscores Iran war-oil shock realityPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to work from home, avoid unnecessary travel and return to virtual meetings amid the Gulf crisis has reignited the WFH debate, with several countries already adopting similar fuel-saving measures as the energy shock deepens.

Solid Hindu vote, fractured Muslim vote; did Bengal just invert India's electoral calculus?The Muslim vote bloc, almost taken for granted as rock solid by the 'secular' parties, delivered a shock departure from conventional behaviour in the just-concluded Bengal elections.

Crisis in AIADMK after Tamil Nadu polls, Shanmugam-led faction wants EPS to quitA section of AIADMK MLAs, backed by senior party leader CV Shanmugam, has asked party chief Edappadi K Palaniswami to quit due to repeated electoral defeats, sources said. The AIADMK managed to win only 47 of 234 seats in the Tamil Nadu Assembly polls.

Bengaluru techies jump with joy over PM's mention of WFHPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to corporates to see if they can revive work from home (WFH) to save fuel has triggered an excited conversation among Bengaluru's techies. The reactions range from hopes of no commute to work and more family time to concerns over collaboration as a team, and the local economy.

Bihar's 'salon ATM' gets a makeover as SBI removes logoA former SBI ATM in Bihar's Danapur was turned into a salon while keeping its old kiosk exterior. However, the logo has been removed after the video went viral.

Live: Class 12th results to be out soonCBSE Board 12th Results Live Updates: The CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 is expected to be declared shortly, with over 18 lakh students eagerly awaiting their board exam scores. Students can check their CBSE 12th results online at the CBSE results official website -- results.cbse.nic.in and download their digital marksheet through DigiLocker, where a "Results Coming Soon" banner has already appeared. Get live updates on CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 date, time, direct result link, and DigiLocker marksheet.

No petrol or diesel shortage, govt assures day after PM's 'save fuel' appealDuring an inter-ministerial briefing on the situation in West Asia, government officials said India has enough fuel stocks and has taken steps to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict in the region.

Love is always louder: Trisha shuts down trolls after viral pics from Vijay oathIn an Instagram post, actor Trisha Krishnan made a loud statement amid backlash over her presence at Vijay's oath-taking ceremony on May 10.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayOnce upon a time, Rs 1 crore felt like enough to secure life after retirement. Today, many Indians believe even Rs 10–15 crore may not be enough. I spoke to families to find out what retirement means to them today, and why expectations are rising.

Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

Why are all cricket finals going to Ahmedabad?The BCCI announced the venue for the Indian Premier League 2026 final. No prizes for guessing, that the match was granted to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, much to the dissatisfaction of many fans.

Molested, racially abused, clothes torn: 2 Northeast women allege assault in DelhiA morning altercation near Nehru Place in Delhi has sparked another probe into alleged abuse targeting women from the Northeast, with police identifying four accused and launching raids to trace them.

'Common man' Vijay swaps veshti-shirt for blazer and pants at oathVijay's attire and messaging marked a political turning point in Tamil Nadu, symbolising a generational shift and the end of an era of Dravidian political norms. With Arvind Kejriwal's "aam aadmi" image recently under strain amid controversies, Vijay appears to have filled the vacuum.

Calcutta was the OG aviation hub long before Dubai's riseDubai's aviation hub faces an existential threat from the war in the Middle East. But what many don't know is that before Dubai's rise, India's Calcutta (now Kolkata) was a major global aviation hub. Till the 1960s, Dum Dum Airport was served by almost every major airline to the Americas and continental Europe. Today, not a single direct flight connects to anywhere beyond Southeast Asia.

Vinesh Phogat hits back at Wrestling Federation over comeback controversyVinesh Phogat has publicly responded to WFI's showcause notice, claiming the federation wrongly interpreted her comeback timeline. The wrestler insisted she remains eligible to compete and plans to return to action at the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda.

Vijay and Udhayanidhi's old pic goes viral as they get set for Assembly face-offVijay and Udhayanidhi Stalin have been film industry colleagues for years. They will now face off in the Assembly, with TVK's Vijay as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and Udhaynidhi as Leader of Opposition. An old photograph of the two actors-turned-politicians has now gone viral.

sports.headline: Virat Kohli rates Krunal Pandya's fearless knock vs MI his best ever in IPL IPL 2026: Virat Kohli hailed Krunal Pandya's fighting 73 against Mumbai Indians as one of the best pressure knocks he has seen in the IPL. The RCB star praised Krunal's courage after the all-rounder battled severe cramps to guide Bengaluru to a thrilling win.

PBKS vs DC IPL 2026: Punjab aim to revive campaign, Delhi look for survival PBKS (Punjab) vs DC (Delhi) Live Score, IPL 2026: Punjab Kings have lost three matches on the trot and will be looking to bounce back when they face Delhi Capitals in Dharamsala on Monday. The Capitals, on the other hand, are in a must-win position.

India retain top spot in ICC men's ODI ranking after annual update India held on to the top spot in the ICC Men's ODI team rankings after the annual update, despite slipping by one rating point. The reshuffle also sharpened the World Cup 2027 qualification race, with South Africa's host status making the cut-off more consequential.

South Africa pacer reverses retirement, to be named in Women's T20 World Cup squad Veteran South Africa fast bowler Shabnim Ismail is set to reverse her international retirement and return to the Proteas squad for the Women's T20 World Cup after discussions with team management.

Marcus Rashford to return to Man United? Barca winger drops hint after title triumph Marcus Rashford drove Barcelona towards the title against Real Madrid and then said he would stay if the choice were his. His remarks further point to a permanent split from Manchester United after a season that has reset his standing in Spain.

Vinesh Phogat hits back at Wrestling Federation over comeback controversy Vinesh Phogat has publicly responded to WFI's showcause notice, claiming the federation wrongly interpreted her comeback timeline. The wrestler insisted she remains eligible to compete and plans to return to action at the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda.

Victory scar: Krunal flaunts pic of bruised stomach after heroic knock vs MI IPL 2026: Krunal Pandya battled cramps and blows to make 73 off 46 as RCB chased 167 against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. His innings, marked by pain, poise and defiance, sealed a crucial last-over win for RCB on Sunday.

Vinesh Phogat contests federation ban, arrives for comeback wrestling event Despite the WFI declaring her ineligible, Vinesh Phogat arrived in Gonda for the National Open Ranking tournament and defended her comeback by citing compliance with regulations of word bodies.

George Bailey dismisses concerns after big-name Australian players reject contracts Australia chair of selectors George Bailey played down concerns over a group of senior players rejecting Cricket Australia's initial contract offers, saying tensions around deals and franchise opportunities are "normal" in the current cricket landscape.

Why are all cricket finals going to Ahmedabad? The BCCI announced the venue for the Indian Premier League 2026 final. No prizes for guessing, that the match was granted to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, much to the dissatisfaction of many fans.

Krunal excited about Kohli's slump? RCB star explains bizarre statement Krunal Pandya dismissed concerns over Virat Kohli's two-game run drought and backed him to hit back hard. Krunal said that there is no worry in the RCB camp about Kohli's form.

Peter Schmeichel accuses Arsenal of hypocrisy after controversial VAR callPeter Schmeichel blasted the officials after Arsenal's controversial 1-0 win over West Ham United, claiming the league leaders "would never be top" if similar physical challenges were consistently punished as fouls.

Delhi Capitals accept defeat in play-off race, to act party poopers for othersIPL 2026: Ian Bell says Delhi Capitals want to finish strongly despite an inconsistent season damaging their playoff push. Delhi will be aiming to spoil the party for the rest of the teams as the season comes to a close.

Shahbaz Ahmed leads big-money buys at Bengal T20 League auctionShahbaz Ahmed emerged as the biggest buy at the Bengal T20 League Men's Auction after Shrachi Tribes Rarh Tigers retained the Indian all-rounder for INR 12.20 lakh through the Right To Match card.

How MS Dhoni's advice turned CSK's Urvil Patel into an IPL record breakerUrvil Patel said a dressing-room conversation with MS Dhoni helped him reset before his 13-ball fifty against LSG. The knock powered CSK past LSG and doubled as a tribute to his father, who told him to bat freely.

No IPL stars in Australia squad for Pakistan ODIs, some return for Bangladesh tourCricket Australia named separate white-ball squads for Pakistan and Bangladesh under Mitchell Marsh. Pat Cummins, Mitchell Starc and Josh Hazlewood will miss both the tours, which get underway from May 30.

PBKS vs DC preview: Can Punjab arrest their slide in the hills?PBKS vs DC preview, IPL 2026: With three straight losses and their season on the brink, Punjab Kings seek a mountain-air reset in Dharamshala. They face a demoralised Delhi Capitals, desperate to play the spoiler and salvage some tattered pride.

IPL 2026: Did Mumbai Indians bring back the wrong Pandya?IPL 2026: While Hardik Pandya has been captaining a sinking ship at Mumbai Indians, his older brother Krunal is quietly, steadily becoming the most indispensable match winner at Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

RCB coach Flower given hefty fine for fiery umpire confrontation during MI clashAndy Flower was slapped with a hefty fine after he confronted the fourth umpire after a disputed boundary call during RCB's win over MI. The fine settled a Level 1 code breach, but the flashpoint underlined the strain around high-stakes decisions.

Did David give MI middle finger salute in Raipur tie? RCB star's viral videoA video appearing to show Tim David making an obscene gesture after RCB's win over MI has gone viral. RCB were able to edge past MI to steal the win in Raipur and move to the top of the table.

Bring back Bhuvneshwar Kumar: World Cup winners call for RCB star's India returnBhuvneshwar Kumar's four wickets and a late six in RCB's win over Mumbai Indians triggered fresh calls for an India recall from Kris Srikkanth and R Ashwin. His control with the new ball and at the death has turned a strong IPL season into a serious selection argument.

Emotional Flick fights back tears after El Clasico win, mourns father's deathAn emotional Hansi Flick held back tears after El Clasico win over Real Madrid, revealing that his father had passed away on the eve of the game. Barca defeated Madrid 2-0 to clinch the LaLiga title.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: Bhuvneshwar's six trumps his four in Raipur nail-biterIPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's four-wicket haul had already put RCB in control against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. But it was his pressure six in the final over that ultimately decided the May 10 thriller and sealed a dramatic two-wicket win.

I don't think we deserved to win: Patidar after RCB win last-ball thriller vs MIIPL 2026: Rajat Patidar admitted Royal Challengers Bengaluru made hard work of a chase they should have controlled against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. Krunal Pandya's rescue and Bhuvneshwar Kumar's late blow sent RCB top and ended Mumbai's playoff hopes.

Krunal Pandya special knocks MI out as RCB win epic last-ball thrillerRoyal Challengers Bengaluru dethroned SunRisers Hyderabad from the top of the IPL points table. It did not come easy as RCB were stretched to the final ball of the match, where they eventually clinched a low-scoring thriller vs MI.

Hardik Pandya unfollows MI on Insta as fallout rumours growIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya briefly appeared to unfollow Mumbai Indians on Instagram after MI's playoff exit. The account returned within minutes, but the episode intensified speculation around his future.

Krunal Pandya tears up remembering good old MI days with Pollard and HardikKrunal Pandya made 73 through cramps to steer Royal Challengers Bengaluru past Mumbai Indians in a final-ball chase. The innings knocked Mumbai out of the IPL 2026 playoff race and brought back memories of his bond with Hardik Pandya and Kieron Pollard.

Controversial VAR tilts Premier League title race in Arsenal's favourArsenal beat West Ham after VAR ruled out Callum Wilson's stoppage-time equaliser for contact on David Raya. The decision inflamed accusations of inconsistency and sharpened pressure at both ends of the table.

India U17 women eye historic World Cup dream in Asian Cup quarterfinal vs ChinaIndia's U17 women face hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal on Monday. A win would send India to the 2026 FIFA U17 Women's World Cup in Morocco through qualification for the first time.

Virat Kohli milestone IPL night ends in golden duck heartbreakIPL 2026: Virat Kohli's 278th IPL appearance ended with a first-ball duck as he miscued Deepak Chahar to mid-off against Mumbai Indians. The failure made it successive ducks for Kohli, a rare slump on a night that should have marked a milestone.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
